Event

APRE Conference

October 27 - 29
Nashville, TN
Register

The American Hospital Association’s Allied Public Relations Executives (APRE) meeting brings together leading voices in healthcare communications, public relations, marketing, and brand development. APRE members are key decision makers shaping how hospitals and health systems engage their stakeholders and communities.

BPD will be in attendance and we’re excited to connect. As a strategic partner to healthcare’s leading brands, we look forward to sharing our latest thinking, learning from industry peers, and exploring how we can help you move faster, think smarter, and make a greater impact in the communities you serve.

October 29, 2025, 9:30 - 10:00 AM CT

Hospital Reputation Attacks – What’s Trending and How do We Combat it?

Hospitals and health systems have long been targeted by negative narratives that portray them as profit-driven entities prioritizing revenue over patient care. As insurance premiums rise and administrative burdens increase, blame for healthcare costs has shifted to hospitals. This narrative, fueled by media campaigns, academic research, and external pressures, has eroded consumer trust, with many believing hospitals care more about profits than patients. With mounting political and reputational risks, it's time for hospitals to unite and defend their commitment to quality patient care.

Featured Speaker

Mike Petrone, VP Communications

Learn more about session

BPDerrrs attending

Erin Steuben, BPD VP, Communications (Growth) Mike Petrone, BPD VP, Communications
Erin Steuben, BPD VP, Communications (Growth) LinkedIn

Erin holds a Bachelor’s degree in Communication from Nebraska Wesleyan University and has over 12 years of experience in transforming healthcare experiences. With a strong passion for utilizing data and technology to enhance brand strategies, Erin excels in building relationships, identifying clients’ most critical challenges, and aligning tailored solutions to drive success.

Outside of her professional work, Erin enjoys spending time with her husband, two children, and their miniature dachshund. Her personal interests include travel, Taylor Swift, and NASCAR, reflecting her diverse passions and dynamic approach to both life and work.

Mike Petrone, BPD VP, Communications LinkedIn

Mike has over a decade of experience in industries ranging from technology to healthcare to lead client efforts on thought leadership, organizational communications, earned and social media and issues/crises. He has a strong track record in critical thinking and storytelling, helping clients leverage communications as a way to function more effectively and ensure alignment both internally and externally. When Mike is not working to protect or promote a client’s reputation, he can be found spending time with his wife Megan and their dog Meatball, working on his golf game or, more likely, praying that the Buffalo Bills will win a Super Bowl at some point in his life.

Tomorrow Is Too Late

The growing perception that hospitals prioritize revenue over patients is now defining the industry. Health systems must do more — now — to address these threats. Tomorrow is too late.
